zh.lifeSummary: 當Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach於1443年2月2日出生在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 明陳時，她的父親Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach41歲，母親Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck29歲。她於1460年11月19日與Ernst von Sachsen在德國薩辛萊格 提福藍斯布特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1484年2月23日在德國薩辛萊格享年41歲，埋葬在德國薩辛萊格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach于1443年2月2日出生在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 明陳时，她的父亲Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach41岁，母亲Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck29岁。她于1460年11月19日与Ernst von Sachsen在德国薩辛萊格 提福藍斯布特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1484年2月23日在德国薩辛萊格享年41岁，埋葬在德国薩辛萊格。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Bayern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German (and possibly also Dutch)   Bayer   or   Beyers  , or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Byers  .

variants.name: Bayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt, Otto, Klaus, Manfred, Bernd, Bernhard, Erwin, Franz, Hans, Johann, Juergen.

German, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Bavaria (German Bayern). This region of southern Germany derives its name from that of the Celtic tribe of the Boii who once inhabited this area. They were displaced in the 6th century AD by an ancient Germanic people, the Boioarii or Baiuarii, whose name is derived from that of their Celtic predecessors. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, and Poland. Compare Beyer and Payer .

English (Lincolnshire): occupational name for a maker of baize cloth, from an agent derivative of Old French baies, Middle English bayes (from the adjective bai ‘reddish-brown, bay’), probably so called because of its original color. This material was said to have been introduced into Britain by immigrants from France and the Netherlands in the 16th century, but the word certainly appears much earlier in English. The surname may also be topographic denoting a ‘dweller by the bend’, from an agent derivative of Old English bēag ‘bend’.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach was born on 2 February 1443, in München, Bavaria, Germany, her father, Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach, was 41 and her mother, Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck, was 29. She married Ernst von Sachsen on 19 November 1460, in Leipziger Tieflandsbucht, Saxony, Germany. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 23 February 1484, in Leipzig, Saxony, Germany, at the age of 41, and was buried in Leipzig, Saxony, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach est née le 2 février 1443, à Landkreis München, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Ernst von Sachsen le 19 novembre 1460, à Leipziger Tieflandsbucht, Sachsen, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 février 1484, à Leipzig, Sachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterrée à Leipzig, Sachsen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach am 2. Februar 1443 in Landkreis München, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach 41 und ihre Mutter Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck 29. Sie hat am 19. November 1460 in Leipziger Tieflandsbucht, Sachsen, Deutschland Ernst von Sachsen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 23. Februar 1484 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Leipzig, Sachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Leipzig, Sachsen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach è nata il il 2 febbraio 1443 nel Landkreis München, Bayern, Germania, sua padre, Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Ernst von Sachsen il 19 novembre 1460, nel Leipziger Tieflandsbucht, Sachsen, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 23 febbraio 1484, nel Leipzig, Sachsen, Germania, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolta a Leipzig, Sachsen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbachが1443年2月2日にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス ムuンチェンで生まれた時，父Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbachは41歳，母Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeckは29歳であった。1460年11月19日にドイツサチセンレイプズイジャー ティエフランドスブチトでErnst von Sachsenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1484年2月23日にドイツサチセンレイプズイグで41歳で死去し，ドイツサチセンレイプズイグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach(이)가 1443년 2월 2일 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 u천에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1460년 11월 19일 독일 작센 레이p지거 티플랜드스버트에서 Ernst von Sachsen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1484년 2월 23일 독일 작센 레이p지그에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 작센 레이p지그에 묻혔다.

pt.2 February 1443–23 February 1484: 2 de fevereiro de 1443–23 de fevereiro de 1484
pt.Nannhofen, Furstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Ger: Nannhofen, Mammendorf, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemanha
pt.Meißen, Kreis Minden, Provinz Westfalen,: Meißen, Kreis Minden, Provinz Westfalen, Preußen, Alemanha
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach nasceu em 2 de fevereiro de 1443, em Landkreis München, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Ernst von Sachsen em 19 de novembro de 1460, em Leipziger Tieflandsbucht, Sachsen, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 23 de fevereiro de 1484, em Leipzig, Sachsen, Alemanha, com 41 anos, e foi sepultada em Leipzig, Sachsen, Alemanha.

ru.Nannhofen, Furstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Ger: наннофен, маммендорф, ландкрис фuрстенфелдбрукк, беиирн, Германия
ru.Meißen, Kreis Minden, Provinz Westfalen,: миssен, крис минден, провинз вестфеилен, Пруссия, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach родилась 2 февраля 1443 г. в местности ландкрис мuнчэн, беиирн, Германия, её отцу по имени Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ernst von Sachsen 19 ноября 1460 г. в местности липзигир тиефландсбучт, сачсен, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 23 февраля 1484 г. в местности липзиг, сачсен, Германия в возрасте 41 год и была похоронена в местности липзиг, сачсен, Германия.

es.Nannhofen, Furstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Ger: Nannhofen, Mammendorf, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemania
es.Meißen, Kreis Minden, Provinz Westfalen,: Meißen, Kreis Minden, Provinz Westfalen, Preußen, Alemania
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Prinzessin Elisabeth von Bayern Wittelsbach nació el 2 de febrero de 1443, en Landkreis München, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Herzog Albrecht III von Bayern Wittelsbach, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Anna Princess von Braunschweig-Grubenhagen-Einbeck, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Ernst von Sachsen el 19 de noviembre de 1460, en Leipziger Tieflandsbucht, Sachsen, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 23 de febrero de 1484, en Leipzig, Sachsen, Alemania, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultada en Leipzig, Sachsen, Alemania.
